This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}
2026-04-03T01:02:13.5105218Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-03T01:01:19.825688488Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get a specific label from a repository."},"description":"Get a specific label from a repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"name":{"description":"Label name.","type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner (username or organization name)","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo","name"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_label"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get issue details"},"description":"Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"issue_number":{"description":"The number of the issue","type":"number"},"method":{"description":"The read operation to perform on a single issue.\nOptions are:\n1. get - Get details of a specific issue.\n2. get_comments - Get issue comments.\n3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.\n4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.\n","enum":["get","get_comments","get_sub_issues","get_labels"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"The owner of the repository","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"The name of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"issue_read"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List available issue types"},"description":"List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"The organization owner of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["owner"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issue_types"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List issues"},"description":"List issues in a GitHub repository. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. 2026-04-03T01:02:13.5167754Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-03T01:01:54.349614821Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"57755","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-03T01:02:13.5194145Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-03T01:01:54.55626541Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57755,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Backward-compat model factory overloads produce malformed `global::.TypeName` for flattened property types\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen the mgmt generator creates backward-compatibility model factory overloads for models with `@flattenProperty`, the generated code contains malformed type references like `global::.StorageTaskReportProperties` (note the empty namespace between `global::` and `.TypeName`). This produces `CS1001: Identifier expected` compilation errors.\\n\\n## Affected SDK\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Storage` — methods `StorageTaskReportInstance` and `FileServiceUsageData` in the generated `ArmStorageModelFactory.cs`.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\nThe bug manifests only when **all three** conditions are met:\\n\\n1. A model with `@flattenProperty` on a `properties` envelope (e.g., `@@flattenProperty(Foo.properties)`)\\n2. The `FlattenPropertyVisitor` replaces the `properties` parameter with individual flattened parameters in the primary factory method\\n3. A **baseline contract DLL** (previous GA release) exists whose model factory still has the pre-flattening method signature with the `properties` parameter\\n\\n### Minimal TypeSpec pattern (already exists in test project)\\n\\nThe `Mgmt-TypeSpec` test project at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/TestProjects/Local/Mgmt-TypeSpec/` already has the exact pattern in `foo.tsp` + `main.tsp`:\\n\\n```typespec\\n// foo.tsp — model with properties envelope\\nmodel FooProperties {\\n  serviceUrl?: url;\\n  something: string;\\n  boolValue?: boolean;\\n  // ...\\n}\\n\\n@resource(\\u0026#34;foos\\u0026#34;)\\nmodel Foo is TrackedResource { ... }\\n\\n// main.tsp — apply flatten\\n@@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.flattenProperty(Foo.properties);\\n```\\n\\nThe test project does **not** have a baseline DLL, so the bug does not manifest there. To reproduce:\\n1. Generate the test project **without** `@flattenProperty` → model factory has `FooProperties properties` parameter\\n2. Package/set output as baseline contract\\n3. Re-generate **with** `@flattenProperty` → primary method now has flattened individual params\\n4. `ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility` creates backward-compat overload using the contract\\u0026#39;s parameter types\\n5. If the contract\\u0026#39;s `FooProperties` type can\\u0026#39;t be resolved by Roslyn (missing assembly ref), its `CSharpType.Namespace` is `\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;`\\n6. `CodeWriter.AppendType` outputs `global::.FooProperties`\\n\\n### Broken output example (from Storage)\\n\\n```csharp\\n// CS1001: Identifier expected — empty namespace after global::\\npublic static StorageTaskReportInstance StorageTaskReportInstance(\\n    ResourceIdentifier id = default, string name = default,\\n    ResourceType resourceType = default, SystemData systemData = default,\\n    global::.StorageTaskReportProperties properties = default)\\n//  ^^^^^^^^ malformed — should be StorageTaskReportProperties or global::Azure.ResourceManager.Storage.Models.StorageTaskReportProperties\\n```\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\n### 1. `CodeWriter.AppendType` — no guard for empty namespace\\n`Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator.dll` → `CodeWriter.AppendType()`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// When type.Namespace == \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, this produces \\u0026#34;global::.TypeName\\u0026#34;\\nUseNamespace(type.Namespace);\\nAppendRaw(\\u0026#34;global::\\u0026#34;);\\nAppendRaw(type.Namespace);   // ← empty string\\nAppendRaw(\\u0026#34;.\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### 2. `ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol` — empty namespace from unresolved types\\n`Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator.dll` → `TypeSymbolExtensions.ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol()`\\n\\n```csharp\\nstring[] array = fullyQualifiedName.Split(\\u0026#39;.\\u0026#39;); // [\\u0026#34;StorageTaskReportProperties\\u0026#34;] — no dots\\nstring ns = string.Join(\\u0026#39;.\\u0026#39;, array.Take(array.Length - 1));  // Take(1, 0) → \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n```\\n\\nWhen the Roslyn compilation can\\u0026#39;t resolve a type from the baseline DLL (e.g., the assembly reference is missing), `GetFullyQualifiedName()` may return just the type name without its namespace. `ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol` then produces a `CSharpType` with `Namespace = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;`.\\n\\n### 3. Pipeline ordering prevents mgmt-level fix\\n```\\nStep 1: EnsureBuilt()              → Factory methods created with properties param\\nStep 2: Visitors                   → FlattenPropertyVisitor replaces properties with individual params\\nStep 3: FilterCustomizedMembers\\nStep 4: ProcessTypeForBackCompat   → Creates backward-compat overloads (BUG: uses contract types with empty ns)\\nStep 5: CodeWriter.Write()         → Outputs global::.TypeName\\n```\\n\\n`FlattenPropertyVisitor` runs in Step 2, so it cannot fix backward-compat overloads created in Step 4.\\n\\n## Suggested Fixes\\n\\n### Option A: Defensive guard in `CodeWriter.AppendType` (base generator)\\n```csharp\\nif (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(type.Namespace))\\n{\\n    AppendRaw(\\u0026#34;global::\\u0026#34;);\\n    AppendRaw(type.Namespace);\\n    AppendRaw(\\u0026#34;.\\u0026#34;);\\n}\\nelse\\n{\\n    // Fall back to unqualified name\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Option B: Guard in `ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol` (base generator)\\n```csharp\\nstring ns = array.Length \\u0026gt; 1\\n    ? string.Join(\\u0026#39;.\\u0026#39;, array.Take(array.Length - 1))\\n    : string.Empty;\\n\\n// If ns is empty and the type is an error type, try resolving from containing namespace\\n```\\n\\n### Option C: Post-backward-compat fix in mgmt generator\\nOverride `BuildMethodsForBackCompatibility` or `GetWriter` in the mgmt generator to detect and fix parameter types with empty namespaces before writing.\\n\\n## Environment\\n- **Base generator**: `Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator` v1.0.0-alpha.20260331.4\\n- **Mgmt generator**: `Azure.Generator.Management` (local)\\n- **Affected package**: `Azure.ResourceManager.Storage` (TypeSpec migration from Swagger/AutoRest)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57755\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-03T00:59:33Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-03T00:59:33Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-03T01:02:13.5216301Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-03T01:01:54.568681408Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57755,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-03T01:02:13.5242531Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-03T01:01:54.778972994Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57755,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Backward-compat model factory overloads produce malformed `global::.TypeName` for flattened property types\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen the mgmt generator creates backward-compatibility model factory overloads for models with `@flattenProperty`, the generated code contains malformed type references like `global::.StorageTaskReportProperties` (note the empty namespace between `global::` and `.TypeName`). This produces `CS1001: Identifier expected` compilation errors.\\n\\n## Affected SDK\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Storage` — methods `StorageTaskReportInstance` and `FileServiceUsageData` in the generated `ArmStorageModelFactory.cs`.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n### Prerequisites\\nThe bug manifests only when **all three** conditions are met:\\n\\n1. The `FlattenPropertyVisitor` replaces the `properties` parameter with individual flattened parameters in the primary factory method\\n3. A **baseline contract DLL** (previous GA release) exists whose model factory still has the pre-flattening method signature with the `properties` parameter\\n\\n### Minimal TypeSpec pattern (already exists in test project)\\n\\nThe `Mgmt-TypeSpec` test project at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/TestProjects/Local/Mgmt-TypeSpec/` already has the exact pattern in `foo.tsp` + `main.tsp`:\\n\\n```typespec\\n// foo.tsp — model with properties envelope\\nmodel FooProperties {\\n  serviceUrl?: url;\\n  something: string;\\n  boolValue?: boolean;\\n  // ...\\n}\\n\\n@resource(\\u0026#34;foos\\u0026#34;)\\nmodel Foo is TrackedResource { ... }\\n\\n// main.tsp — apply flatten\\n@@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.flattenProperty(Foo.properties);\\n```\\n\\nThe test project does **not** have a baseline DLL, so the bug does not manifest there. To reproduce:\\n1. Generate the test project **without** `@flattenProperty` → model factory has `FooProperties properties` parameter\\n2. Package/set output as baseline contract\\n3. Re-generate **with** `@flattenProperty` → primary method now has flattened individual params\\n4. `ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility` creates backward-compat overload using the contract\\u0026#39;s parameter types\\n5. If the contract\\u0026#39;s `FooProperties` type can\\u0026#39;t be resolved by Roslyn (missing assembly ref), its `CSharpType.Namespace` is `\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;`\\n6. `CodeWriter.AppendType` outputs `global::.FooProperties`\\n\\n### Broken output example (from Storage)\\n\\n```csharp\\n// CS1001: Identifier expected — empty namespace after global::\\npublic static StorageTaskReportInstance StorageTaskReportInstance(\\n    ResourceIdentifier id = default, string name = default,\\n    ResourceType resourceType = default, SystemData systemData = default,\\n    global::.StorageTaskReportProperties properties = default)\\n//  ^^^^^^^^ malformed — should be StorageTaskReportProperties or global::Azure.ResourceManager.Storage.Models.StorageTaskReportProperties\\n```\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\n### 1. `CodeWriter.AppendType` — no guard for empty namespace\\n`Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator.dll` → `CodeWriter.AppendType()`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// When type.Namespace == \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, this produces \\u0026#34;global::.TypeName\\u0026#34;\\nUseNamespace(type.Namespace);\\nAppendRaw(\\u0026#34;global::\\u0026#34;);\\nAppendRaw(type.Namespace);   // ← empty string\\nAppendRaw(\\u0026#34;.\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### 2. `ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol` — empty namespace from unresolved types\\n`Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator.dll` → `TypeSymbolExtensions.ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol()`\\n\\n```csharp\\nstring[] array = fullyQualifiedName.Split(\\u0026#39;.\\u0026#39;); // [\\u0026#34;StorageTaskReportProperties\\u0026#34;] — no dots\\nstring ns = string.Join(\\u0026#39;.\\u0026#39;, array.Take(array.Length - 1));  // Take(1, 0) → \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n```\\n\\nWhen the Roslyn compilation can\\u0026#39;t resolve a type from the baseline DLL (e.g., the assembly reference is missing), `GetFullyQualifiedName()` may return just the type name without its namespace. `ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol` then produces a `CSharpType` with `Namespace = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;`.\\n\\n### 3. Pipeline ordering prevents mgmt-level fix\\n```\\nStep 1: EnsureBuilt()              → Factory methods created with properties param\\nStep 2: Visitors                   → FlattenPropertyVisitor replaces properties with individual params\\nStep 3: FilterCustomizedMembers\\nStep 4: ProcessTypeForBackCompat   → Creates backward-compat overloads (BUG: uses contract types with empty ns)\\nStep 5: CodeWriter.Write()         → Outputs global::.TypeName\\n```\\n\\n`FlattenPropertyVisitor` runs in Step 2, so it cannot fix backward-compat overloads created in Step 4.\\n\\n## Suggested Fixes\\n\\n### Option A: Defensive guard in `CodeWriter.AppendType` (base generator)\\n```csharp\\nif (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(type.Namespace))\\n{\\n    AppendRaw(\\u0026#34;global::\\u0026#34;);\\n    AppendRaw(type.Namespace);\\n    AppendRaw(\\u0026#34;.\\u0026#34;);\\n}\\nelse\\n{\\n    // Fall back to unqualified name\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Option B: Guard in `ConstructCSharpTypeFromSymbol` (base generator)\\n```csharp\\nstring ns = array.Length \\u0026gt; 1\\n    ? string.Join(\\u0026#39;.\\u0026#39;, array.Take(array.Length - 1))\\n    : string.Empty;\\n\\n// If ns is empty and the type is an error type, try resolving from containing namespace\\n```\\n\\n### Option C: Post-backward-compat fix in mgmt generator\\nOverride `BuildMethodsForBackCompatibility` or `GetWriter` in the mgmt generator to detect and fix parameter types with empty namespaces before writing.\\n\\n## Environment\\n- **Base generator**: `Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator` v1.0.0-alpha.20260331.4\\n- **Mgmt generator**: `Azure.Generator.Management` (local)\\n- **Affected package**: `Azure.ResourceManager.Storage` (TypeSpec migration from Swagger/AutoRest)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57755\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-03T00:59:33Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-03T00:59:33Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
